name: Gaussian Quadrature Order Conditions Equivalence
proof_text:
Proof. The first two results (342j), (342k) are consequences of the order
conditions. Given that C(s) is true, all order conditions based on trees
containing the structure · · · [τ k−1 ] · · · , with k ≤ s, can be removed, as we




                                    C(s)

                                                                     G(2s)
                           ∧           ∧

                                              E(s, s)
                  B(2s)                                         ∧

                                                 ∧
                           ∧

                                    D(s)


              Figure 342(i)     Schema representing Theorem 342C



saw in Subsection 321. Similarly, the condition D(s) enables us to remove
from consideration all trees of the form [τ k−1 [· · · ]]. Hence, if both C(s) and
D(s) are true, the only trees remaining are those associated with the trees
covered by B(2s). Hence, (342l) follows. Multiply the matrix of quantities
that must be zero according to the C(s) condition
          "               "                             "                     
              j a1j − c1       a1j cj − 12 c21    ···                 − 1s cs1
                                                                  s−1
                                                          j a1j cj
          "               "j                            "                     
            j a2j − c2       j a2j cj − 2 c2      ···                 − 1s cs2 
                                         1 2                      s−1
                                                         j a2j cj
                                                                              
                ..                ..                              ..          
                 .                 .                               .          
           "               "                             "
             j asj − cs      j asj cj − 2 cs      ···                 − s cs
                                        1 2                       s−1   1 s
                                                           j asj cj

by the non-singular matrix
                                                                
                            b1         b2        ···       bs
                                                                
                        b1 c1       b2 c2       ···     bs cs 
                                                                
                            ..         ..                  ..   
                             .          .                   .   
                         b1 cs−1
                              1     b2 cs−1
                                         2       ···    bs css−1


and the result is the matrix of E(s, s) conditions. Hence, (342m) follows and,
because the matrix multiplier is non-singular, (342n) also follows. The final
results (342o) and (342p) are proved in similar way.

variables.name: G(η)
variables.definition: represents that a given Runge–Kutta method has order η

variables.name: B(2s)
variables.definition: order conditions for Runge–Kutta methods, specifically the quadrature conditions up to order 2s

variables.name: C(s)
variables.definition: simplifying conditions related to the internal stages of the Runge–Kutta method

variables.name: D(s)
variables.definition: simplifying conditions related to the internal stages of the Runge–Kutta method

variables.name: E(s, s)
variables.definition: a condition given by equation (321c) that links C(s) and D(s)

equations.content: equation defining E(s, s) (exact form not provided in the preceding text, but it is referenced as the link between C(s) and D(s))

preamble: The theorem concerns relationships between order conditions for Runge–Kutta methods. The notation G(η) indicates a method of order η, while B, C, D, and E are specific sets of simplifying conditions. The context involves analyzing whether C(s) and D(s) can be satisfied simultaneously given quadrature conditions.
